root_cause.text: The input sanitization logic in `UrlHelper` only performed a single-pass replacement of `<script>` tags, allowing attackers to bypass the filter and execute Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) attacks by using nested or malformed tags (e.g., `<<script>script>`).
root_cause.evidence: The commit message explicitly states "recursively replace <<<<<script>>>>> until completely sanitized", and the patch changes an `if` statement (`if (result.indexOf("<script>") >= 0)`) to a `while` loop (`while (result.indexOf("<script>") > 0)`) to ensure all instances of the tag are removed.

agent_logs.root_cause_candidate: The input sanitization logic in `UrlHelper` only performed a single-pass replacement of `<script>` tags, allowing attackers to bypass the filter and execute Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) attacks by using nested or malformed tags (e.g., `<<script>script>`).
agent_logs.evidence_points: The commit message explicitly states "recursively replace <<<<<script>>>>> until completely sanitized", and the patch changes an `if` statement (`if (result.indexOf("<script>") >= 0)`) to a `while` loop (`while (result.indexOf("<script>") > 0)`) to ensure all instances of the tag are removed.

intent_record.intent_summary: Change script tag sanitization from a single pass to a while loop to handle nested script tags.
intent_record.what: Changed an if condition to a while loop and modified the index check condition.
intent_record.how: Iteratively replaces script tags to ensure nested or constructed tags are fully removed.

grouping_result.intent: Change script tag sanitization from a single pass to a while loop to handle nested script tags.

relevance_review.reason: The group directly fixes the single-pass sanitization issue by changing it to a while loop to properly handle nested script tags, which is the exact root cause of the vulnerability.

candidates.rationale: This line performs the single-pass replacement of '<script>' tags, which allows attackers to bypass the filter using nested tags like '<<script>script>' as described in the root cause.
This line contains the single check for the '<script>' tag, which fails to adequately handle nested tags when paired with the single-pass replacement.

vuln_statements.explanation: This statement performs the single-pass replacement of '<script>' tags, which allows attackers to bypass the filter using nested tags like '<<script>script>' as described in the root cause.
This statement contains the single check for the '<script>' tag, which fails to adequately handle nested tags when paired with the single-pass replacement.

bic_trace_step.commit_msg:
WW-2414 - failsafe sanitization of querystring params

git-svn-id: https://svn.apache.org/repos/asf/struts/struts2/trunk
bic_trace_step.code_snippet:
commit 09147ffad2b3046ed21af0f524c5088e2ac551e6
Author: James Mitchell <jmitchell@apache.org>
Date:   Thu Jan 24 07:39:45 2008 +0000

    WW-2414 - failsafe sanitization of querystring params
    
    git-svn-id: https://svn.apache.org/repos/asf/struts/struts2/trunk@614814 13f79535-47bb-0310-9956-ffa450edef68

diff --git a/core/src/main/java/org/apache/struts2/views/util/UrlHelper.java b/core/src/main/java/org/apache/struts2/views/util/UrlHelper.java
index 019b28872..5b6ca1f53 100644
--- a/core/
bic_trace_step.reason: The commit introduces the vulnerable single-pass replacement logic: `if (result.indexOf("<script>") >= 0){ result = result.replaceAll("<script>", "script"); }`. This allows bypassing the filter using nested tags like `<<script>script>`.
